steps.title: Determine if the function is increasing or decreasing
steps.content: Scanning from left to right along the $x$-axis, the graph rises—$y$-values increase as $x$ increases. The function is increasing. This eliminates choices B (Decreasing linear) and D (Decreasing exponential).

steps.title: Determine if the curve is linear or exponential
steps.content: A linear function would be a straight line with constant slope. The graph shows a curve that is nearly flat on the left but becomes increasingly steep on the right. This accelerating slope is not constant, eliminating choice A (Increasing linear). The pattern of an exponential function shows exactly this type of acceleration—the curve gets steeper and steeper as $x$ increases. That matches choice C — C is correct.
